skill.name: longbridge-value-investing
skill.description:
Value investing analysis using Graham (NCAV/net-net/defensive-investor) and Buffett (economic moat/ROE/FCF) methodologies. Covers single-stock diagnostics and batch screening for both Graham cigar-butt and Buffett quality-compounder criteria. Runs cross-statement reconciliation before scoring. Data from Longbridge CLI first, MCP fallback, WebSearch only for genuine gaps.
Triggers: "格雷厄姆", "巴菲特", "捡烟蒂", "烟蒂股", "NCAV", "净流动资产", "护城河", "价值投资", "安全边际", "深度价值", "撿煙蒂", "煙蒂股", "淨流動資產", "護城河", "安全邊際", "Graham", "Buffett", "cigar butt", "net-net", "NCAV screen", "moat", "value investing", "margin of safety", "deep value", "quality compounder", "價值投資", "深度價值", "防御型投资者", "防禦型投資者"
skill.summary: Graham and Buffett value investing analysis — NCAV screening, economic moat diagnostics, and cross-statement reconciliation via Longbridge.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: All 176 static findings are false positives. This is a prompt-only markdown documentation skill with no executable code. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings (158 instances) are triggered by backtick characters in markdown code blocks containing CLI command examples (e.g. `longbridge quote <SYMBOL> --format json`), not actual code execution. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings (18 instances) are false matches on text patterns in documentation. The 'System reconnaissance' findings match on financial sector terms (banks/insurance/REITs), not system commands.

low_findings.title: Markdown backtick syntax misinterpreted as code execution
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged 158 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' across all files. These are false positives — every instance is a backtick-delimited CLI command example in markdown documentation (e.g. `longbridge quote <SYMBOL> --format json`). The skill contains no Ruby, shell scripts, or executable code. The 'external_commands' risk factor is present only in the sense that the skill instructs the LLM to call the longbridge CLI, which is the documented and intended behaviour per the repo's design guidelines.

low_findings.title: Text patterns flagged as weak cryptographic algorithm
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged 18 instances of 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' across reference files. These are false positives triggered by text patterns in markdown documentation that incidentally match crypto-related regex patterns. The skill does not use any cryptographic functions or algorithms — it is a pure prompt-only analysis skill.

low_findings.title: Financial sector terms flagged as system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Two instances of 'System reconnaissance' were flagged (graham-screener.md:82, graham-stock-analysis.md:75). These are false positives — the matched text describes financial sector categories (banks, insurance, REITs, pure-holding shells) and reconciliation tolerances, not system-level reconnaissance.

content.user_title: Analyze Stocks with Graham & Buffett Value Frameworks
content.value_statement: Investors struggle to apply Graham and Buffett methodologies systematically across stocks. This skill provides structured single-stock diagnostics and batch screening using NCAV, economic moat, ROE, and FCF criteria with cross-statement reconciliation.
actual_capabilities: Graham single-stock analysis with 100-point scoring on NCAV, PE, PB, dividend, debt coverage, and earnings stability
Graham batch screener for NCAV/net-net/defensive-investor filters across an index or market universe
Buffett economic moat diagnostic across five dimensions: business, financial health, management, valuation, and long-term visibility
Buffett quality-compounder screener with hard quant filters (ROE ≥ 15%, debt ≤ 50%, FCF positive, gross margin ≥ 30%)
Cross-statement reconciliation (勾稽校验) before scoring with tolerance checks and halt-on-failure logic
limitations: Not suitable for pre-revenue, heavy-loss, or lifecycle-stage-zero companies — Buffett framework requires a track record
Banks, insurance, REITs, and pure-financial holding companies are excluded from NCAV screening as the framework is not applicable
ST stocks, suspended stocks, and IPOs listed less than 2 years receive pro-rated scores with data-limitation flags
Requires longbridge CLI or MCP server for financial data — no direct API integration

use_cases.title: Graham NCAV screen for cigar-butt candidates
use_cases.description: Screen a market universe for stocks trading below net current asset value (NCAV) to identify classic Graham cigar-butt opportunities with margin of safety.
use_cases.target_user: Deep-value investors following Benjamin Graham's net-net methodology

use_cases.title: Buffett-style moat analysis on a single stock
use_cases.description: Get a five-dimension moat diagnostic (business, financials, management, valuation, visibility) with a Buffett-voice narrative and holding-period expectation for a specific ticker.
use_cases.target_user: Quality-compounder investors evaluating long-term ownership candidates

use_cases.title: Cross-statement reconciliation before scoring
use_cases.description: Use the reconciliation gate to verify that fetched financial statements are internally consistent (IS↔BS↔CF links, ROE inputs, market cap) before any value scoring is applied.
use_cases.target_user: Analysts who want data-integrity checks before relying on automated scores

prompt_templates.title: Graham single-stock diagnostic
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze 600519.SH using the Graham defensive-investor framework. Show NCAV, PE, PB, current ratio, and earnings stability. Output the 100-point static score plus dynamic adjustment.

prompt_templates.title: Buffett moat analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a Buffett moat analysis on AAPL.US. Score all five dimensions — business model, financial health, management, valuation, and long-term visibility. Give a Buffett-voice narrative and holding-period expectation.

prompt_templates.title: Batch NCAV screener
prompt_templates.prompt: Screen all A-share stocks trading below NCAV on the Shanghai exchange. Show top 20 by adjusted cigar-butt score, with Graham buy price and value-trap warnings.

prompt_templates.title: Quality-compounder batch filter
prompt_templates.prompt: Find quality compounders in US large-caps: ROE ≥ 15% for 5 years, debt/equity ≤ 50%, positive FCF, gross margin ≥ 30%. Then apply moat scoring to the top 10 and present 3–5 candidates.

output_examples.input: Analyze 00700.HK with the Buffett moat framework
output_examples.output: Overall Rating: ★★★★☆ — Wide moat. Five-dimension scores: Business ★★★★★, Financial Health ★★★★☆, Management ★★★★☆, Valuation ★★★☆☆, Visibility ★★★★★. Holding period: 3–5 years minimum. Buffett-voice summary: Tencent's network-effect moat and regulatory risk offset by strong FCF generation. Data Source Appendix with reconciliation summary line (all checks within tolerance).

output_examples.input: Run NCAV screen on the Hang Seng index
output_examples.output: Universe: 82 stocks. Hard filter pass: 6. Dynamic clean: 5. Top 3 by adjusted cigar-butt score: [ticker, name, NCAV ratio, static score, adjusted score, Graham buy price]. Value-trap warning: 1 candidate flagged for declining NCAV trend. Excluded — NCAV inapplicable: 12 financial-sector stocks. Data Source Appendix with per-row source attribution.

best_practices: Always run the reconciliation gate (勾稽校验) before trusting any score — the skill halts automatically on reconciliation failure > tolerance
Use CLI commands in parallel for speed — fetch BS, IS, and CF statements simultaneously rather than sequentially
Specify the holding-period expectation to the user early — Buffett framework requires a minimum 3-year capital horizon
anti_patterns: Do not apply the Buffett moat framework to pre-revenue or early-stage biotech — use longbridge-fundamental for early-stage views instead
Do not include banks, insurance, or REITs in NCAV screening — the net-net framework is not valid for financial business models
Do not collapse quality and price into a single number — always show the moat verdict and valuation margin-of-safety as separate dimensions

faq.question: What is the difference between Graham and Buffett screening in this skill?
faq.answer: Graham screening focuses on net-net / NCAV / cigar-butt deep-value criteria. Buffett screening focuses on quality compounders with wide economic moats, high ROE, strong FCF, and pricing power. Both share the same cross-statement reconciliation gate.

faq.question: Why are banks and insurance companies excluded from NCAV screening?
faq.answer: NCAV (net current asset value) is not a valid valuation lens for financial business models because their assets and liabilities are fundamentally different from industrial companies. The skill excludes them and points users to longbridge-valuation-methodology instead.

faq.question: What happens if the reconciliation gate fails?
faq.answer: The skill halts scoring entirely when any cross-statement check exceeds tolerance. It emits a halt message naming the failing check and gap, but still prints the data-source appendix and reconciliation summary so the user can see exactly where the data is inconsistent.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Longbridge account or login?
faq.answer: No. All frameworks in this skill use public data — no login required. The longbridge CLI must be installed, or the MCP server must be configured, for the skill to fetch financial data.

faq.question: What is the minimum holding period for Buffett-style picks?
faq.answer: Buffett's reference is ideally forever, with a practical minimum of 3 years. The skill warns explicitly if the user has a shorter capital horizon. Moat width maps to expected hold: five-star moat → 5+ years, four-star → 3–5 years, three-star → 1–3 years.

faq.question: Can I use WebSearch for data that Longbridge does not have?
faq.answer: Yes, but only for items genuinely outside Longbridge: industry outlook, qualitative management signals, brand surveys, and regulatory news. Every WebSearch-sourced figure must be tagged with publisher, date, and URL in the data-source appendix.
